envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Google Workspace connector readiness substrate per ADR-0084. Wave 6 matrix score 13.33 (Workspace TIER_1). Spans Gmail / Calendar / Drive / Docs / Sheets / Slides.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Suggest-only. Activation requires Foundation governance + map-region approval + per-connector RULE 21 research arc completion.

items.MCP_posture: Google Workspace MCP servers exist in the broader MCP ecosystem (community + Google-affiliated). Substrate-honest evaluation deferred to C3 pre-flight. Initial path is direct REST API + read-first scopes via OAuth 2.0 user consent OR domain-wide delegation via service account.
items.OAuth_model: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for per-user delegated access. Service account + JWT for server-to-server with domain-wide delegation (super admin grants the service account permission to act on behalf of users in the domain). Per-API scopes are NOT additive — each scope must be explicitly listed and periodically reviewed per Google best practice.
items.app_installation_model: Google Cloud project + OAuth client + (optionally) Marketplace app listing for org-wide install. Domain-wide delegation registered at Admin Console > Security > Access and data control > API controls > Manage Domain Wide Delegation by super admin.
items.admin_consent_model: Super admin consents at Admin Console for domain-wide delegation scopes. Per-user OAuth flow requires individual user consent unless app is internal + DWD-configured. Marketplace install requires admin approval.
read_capabilities: gmail.metadata (headers + labels only, no message body — lower-sensitivity alternative)
drive.metadata.readonly (Drive file metadata without content — lower-sensitivity alternative)
write_capabilities: gmail.send / gmail.compose (send mail — disabled by default; high-trust write)
calendar.events (create / update events — disabled by default; coordination write)
drive (create / update / share files — disabled by default; data-egress risk)
documents / spreadsheets / presentations write — disabled by default
webhook_or_event_capabilities: Gmail push notifications via Cloud Pub/Sub watch on user mailbox
Calendar push notifications via watch / channels API
Drive changes feed + push notifications via watch / channels API
Webhook endpoints require HTTPS + verification token; integrate with verifyInboundHmac substrate
risky_write_actions: gmail.send to external recipients (legal-effect / external-commit risk)
Calendar event invitations to external attendees
Group / org-unit changes via Admin Directory (admin-scope writes)
required_approval_gates: Map-region approval per ADR-0082 Amendment 1 (Tool Map must approve Google Workspace)
Admin policy approval at activation per ADR-0084
Per-call governance approval for any write action
dual_control_recommendations: External Drive file sharing — dual-control recommended
Admin Directory writes (group / org-unit changes) — dual-control mandatory
Bulk operations (>20 items / window) — second-actor approval
DMW_scope_implications: Per-user Gmail / Calendar / Drive reads scope to user's PERSONAL DMW per ADR-0046 (Personal AI Agent twin context)
Domain-wide delegation reads scope to ENTERPRISE DMW (Enterprise AI Agent context)
Shared Drive content treated as team-scoped DMW
Forbidden categories from Memory / DMW Map (personal / executive / legal / HR) excluded by construction
Gmail metadata-only scope preferred when full body not required (data-minimization)
workflow_purpose_bindings: ADR-0081 Stage 2: Email triage summary recommendations (read-first)
ADR-0081 Stage 2: Calendar conflict / preparation recommendations
ADR-0081 Stage 2: Drive document discovery recommendations
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Drafted email replies awaiting user approval
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Drafted calendar invites awaiting user approval
items.billing_pack_mapping: Workspace / Knowledge Pack family per ADR-0083 Amendment 1 §9.4 (connector-pack.workspace-knowledge.v1).
Dandelion_map_dependencies: Tool Map must approve Google Workspace (sub-tool list: Gmail / Calendar / Drive / Docs)
Workflow Map must approve email / calendar / Drive workflows by purpose
Authority Map must approve send / share / write authority
Memory / DMW Map must approve mail / calendar / Drive memory scope
Risk Map must clear Google Workspace (compliance-sensitive surfaces e.g., HIPAA / FERPA / SOX flagged)
audit_expectations: INVOKE_CONNECTOR + ACTION_* lifecycle at C3+ runtime (existing literals)
Webhook ingestion audited via verifyInboundHmac path
Domain-wide delegation impersonation events surfaced in audit chain (which user was impersonated for which API call)
secret_handling_requirements: OAuth client_secret + service account JSON key via per-tenant secret_ref env-var-NAME pattern per ADR-0024 + ADR-0019
User refresh tokens stored encrypted at ConnectorBinding row; never in logs
Service account private key rotation handled via deployment-target KMS
Token revocation on user offboarding propagated to ConnectorBinding
no_leak_rules: Email body / Drive file content never traverses responses outside the wallet's scope
User email addresses + Drive file IDs never exposed cross-tenant
OAuth client_secret + service account key never logged
Gmail message-IDs scope-checked before any external surface reference
Domain-wide-delegated impersonated-user identity never leaked between Twins
tenant_isolation_requirements: Per-tenant Google Cloud project recommended (or per-tenant OAuth client at minimum)
Domain-wide delegation per customer domain — never shared across customers
ConnectorBinding enforces wallet_id + entity scope on every API call
items.rate_limit_notes: Per-API quotas (Gmail: 1 billion quota units/day, ~250 units/sec/user; Drive: 1,000 req/100sec/user; Calendar: 1,000,000 req/day default). Exponential backoff + jitter required per Google best practice.
testing_strategy: Fixture-based read tests per Google API
Replay-mode integration tests using recorded API responses
Domain-wide delegation impersonation audit assertion tests
No-leak guard tests (no email body / Drive content / tokens in responses)
implementation_risks: Domain-wide delegation is a sensitive primitive — Google admin can grant a service account broad domain-wide access; admin review + audit trail mandatory
Confused-deputy via DWD: service account acting on behalf of users requires per-call user-attribution in audit
OAuth scope sprawl: implementation must keep scopes minimal + periodically reviewed per Google best practice
Gmail vs Gmail metadata scope tradeoff: prefer metadata when business case allows
External Drive sharing surface is a major data-egress risk — keep disabled until C5+
items.first_slice_recommendation: C3 Google Workspace read-first connector runtime — Gmail metadata + Calendar readonly + Drive metadata readonly via OAuth 2.0 per-user delegated tokens. NO writes at C3. Domain-wide delegation deferred to C3.5 (separate Founder authorization). Recommend after C2 Slack + GOVSEC.6 land.
